User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Firebase Blank Slate application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

photoURL.description: The URL of the user's profile picture.

role.description: The role of the user, which determines their permissions.

Content.title: Content
Content.description: Represents content created by a user in the Firebase Blank Slate application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Content entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who created this content. (Relationship: User 1:N Content)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the content was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the content was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a product category to group items.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

description.description: A brief description of the category.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a product in the inventory.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

code.description: A unique, auto-generated code for the product.

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this product belongs to.

supplierId.description: Reference to the Supplier of this product.

minStock.description: The minimum stock level for the product.

unit.description: The unit of measurement for the product stock.

Deposit.title: Deposit
Deposit.description: Represents a physical location where products are stored (e.g., warehouse, storage room).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Deposit entity.

description.description: A brief description or location details of the deposit.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Supplier entity.

phone.description: Contact phone number of the supplier.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity.

name.description: The name of the client or area.

phone.description: Contact phone number of the client.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. User documents are created via Firebase Authentication.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores content created by a user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, representing the owner of the content.

params.name: contentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the content document.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the category.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier of the product.

definition.description: Stores warehouses or physical locations for inventory.

params.name: depositId
params.description: The unique identifier of the deposit.

params.name: supplierId
params.description: The unique identifier of the supplier.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the client.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore data structure is designed to support a simple content creation platform, "Firebase Blank Slate," with user authentication and content storage. The structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and QAPs.

*   **Authorization Independence:** The `Content` documents are stored in a subcollection of the `User` document (`/users/{userId}/content/{contentId}`). The `Content` document includes the `userId` of the owner. This design eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules to determine ownership.
*   **QAPs:** Segregation is achieved by storing all content created by a user within their own `users/{userId}/content` subcollection. This ensures that list operations can be secured by checking `request.auth.uid == userId`. Path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/content/{contentId}`) enables secure and efficient `list` operations.

The data model uses hierarchical paths to express the relationship between users and their content, making the database easier to understand and implement security rules that protect user data.

Authentication is handled by Firebase Authentication, and Firestore is used for content storage.
